premium_vs_free.pm_insight: Discovery pain is roughly equal across tiers (42% Premium vs 38% Free), suggesting the echo chamber issue is a platform-wide algorithmic problem rather than a tier-specific one. This is a critical finding: Premium subscribers are paying €9.99/month and still experiencing the same discovery failures as free users, which directly undermines the premium value proposition.

discovery_questions_answered.why_echo_chambers_form: Echo chambers form through three compounding mechanisms: (1) Collaborative filtering amplifies majority taste signals, suppressing niche preferences. (2) High-playcount signals (from passive/background listening) override engagement signals. (3) The algorithm optimizes for low skip rates, which rewards familiar content. Together these create a self-reinforcing loop where familiarity begets familiarity.
discovery_questions_answered.who_is_most_affected: Loyal long-term Premium users are most severely affected. They have the richest listening history (deepest echo chambers), the highest expectations (paying users), and the most invested emotional relationship with the platform. Genre Explorers are the next most frustrated segment.
discovery_questions_answered.what_features_fail_most: Discover Weekly is the most complained-about feature (62% of discovery complaints). Artist Radio is second (44%), followed by Daily Mix (38%) and Autoplay (31%). Release Radar is the best-performing discovery feature with the fewest complaints.
discovery_questions_answered.free_vs_premium_finding: Discovery pain is roughly equal across tiers (42% Premium vs 38% Free), suggesting the echo chamber issue is a platform-wide algorithmic problem rather than a tier-specific one. This is a critical finding: Premium subscribers are paying €9.99/month and still experiencing the same discovery failures as free users, which directly undermines the premium value proposition.

strategic_recommendations.title: Implement Taste Profile Versioning
strategic_recommendations.description: Allow users to maintain multiple taste profiles (e.g., 'Gym', 'Work', 'Explore') that can be activated contextually. This addresses Contextual Over-reliance (most common root cause) and gives users agency.

strategic_recommendations.title: Introduce Exploration Mode
strategic_recommendations.description: A toggleable mode that temporarily increases the novelty coefficient in all recommendation algorithms. Listening in Exploration Mode does not update the core taste profile. Solves the fear-of-contamination paralysis.

strategic_recommendations.title: Passive vs Active Listening Differentiation
strategic_recommendations.description: Use behavioral signals (skip rate, volume changes, headphone detection, time-of-day) to classify sessions as active or passive. Weight passive sessions less in taste model updates.

strategic_recommendations.title: Echo Chamber Health Score
strategic_recommendations.description: Surface a user-visible 'Discovery Score' showing how diverse their recent listening has been. Gamify music exploration. Users who score low see a prompt to try Exploration Mode.

strategic_recommendations.title: Discover Weekly Quality Gate
strategic_recommendations.description: Before publishing Discover Weekly, run an automated check: no song that appears in user's Liked Songs, no artist the user follows, no song played in the last 90 days. Basic quality control that should already exist.
